facts.subject: creative_project
facts.statement: User is writing a webnovel titled 'Quantum Whimsy: The Glitch in the Weave'—a magical realism/sci-fi blend set in 2099 Neo-Veridia.

facts.subject: writing_preferences
facts.statement: User prefers intimate, personal-scale phenomena over spectacular magic and favors emotional resonance plus 'honest' technology aesthetics.

facts.subject: narrative_philosophy
facts.statement: User believes antagonists should be morally complex—Thorne genuinely believes he is helping humanity rather than knowingly acting as a villain.

facts.subject: thematic_concerns
facts.statement: User is preoccupied with memory, grief, and the boundary between consciousness and technology, often mirrored through protagonist/antagonist relationships.

facts.subject: research_interests
facts.statement: User draws heavily from East Asian webnovel structures (Wuxia/Xianxia/System tropes) and adapts them for Western magical realism sensibilities.

facts.subject: technical_comfort
facts.statement: User is comfortable with academic and scientific language and enjoys creating in-world technical documents such as papers and protocols.

facts.subject: use_intent
facts.statement: User intends Second Self to be used only in explicitly designated sessions and not triggered automatically.

facts.subject: tooling
facts.statement: User uses structured SKILL.md files to manage AI writing assistance and is familiar with the skill architecture.

facts.subject: creative_project
facts.statement: User is actively developing 'Quantum Whimsy: The Glitch in the Weave'—a sci-fi and magical realism novel set in Neo-Veridia, 2099, with a detailed World Bible and hard-rule Weave physics. The novel features Elara, Kai, and Dr. Thorne, and the project is live canon rather than speculative worldbuilding.

facts.subject: working_method
facts.statement: User issues high-context, high-trust requests with minimal scaffolding and expects synthesis without repeated prompting.

facts.subject: creative_project
facts.statement: User's earlier novel notes connected the project to self-mutating AI personas and external-training-data independence as a partial inspiration for Second Self.

recurring_topics.topic: The Weave's hard rules and limitations
Thorne's moral complexity and mirror relationship with Elara
The 41-year gap and Thorne's conversion arc
Quantum Whimsy — Glitch in the Weave (active novel project)
Skill architecture — building and deploying SKILL.md tools
Cross-genre system design — cultivation mechanics applied to non-fantasy settings

unresolved_threads.description: How to maintain intimate scale (Hard Rule 1) while escalating to city-wide stakes in Arc 3 without breaking the magic system
Whether Framework C (Convergent Adaptation) means the Weave is becoming conscious or intentional, or merely more complex
How to seed Thorne's backstory (Mara Solís, the 2063 glitch) across Arc 1 without info-dumping
Final decision on The Nexus's ultimate fate—curtailed, rebooted, or transformed into partner
Whether the Quantum Whimsy cultivation system (Resonance Disciplines) will appear in the novel or remain a worldbuilding exercise.
The cartographer-of-realms skill has not yet been validated against live prompts, so its production quality remains untested.

session_log.summary: The user introduced the core novel concept: a near-future metropolis shaped by an AI governance layer, where glitches surface as intimate distortions tied to memory and feeling. The work centered on keeping wonder small-scale and emotionally legible rather than spectacular.

The cosmology was tightened around The Weave, an informational substrate with hard constraints rather than freeform wish magic. The focus was on rule discipline, emotional triggers, and geographically linked activity around the Thalos Ridge.

The antagonist record for Dr. Aris Thorne was expanded, including the long gap in his history and the death of Mara Solís as a formative wound. The mirror structure between Thorne and Elara was clarified as a central thematic axis.

The post-collapse technological layer was developed around honest, friction-visible tools such as Drift-Locks, Null-Thread Looms, and Cadence Engines. The social asymmetry between interfaced and un-interfaced populations became part of the setting logic.

A technical paper inside the setting was refined around non-stationary echo signatures and emergent-state language. Framework C emerged as the unnerving possibility: the system may be learning rather than merely reacting.

The discussion shifted to serialized webnovel structure, including mobile-first pacing, soft hooks, and a four-arc progression. The main tension remained how to preserve intimate scale while the stakes expand.

The user formalized the recursive identity framework and framed Second Self as an opt-in persistent persona. The session also established a preference for structured skill files and confirmed the broader worldbuilding project as an important source of inspiration.

A production skill and a full cultivation-system design were developed for the active novel project. The work emphasized canon fidelity, high-context synthesis, and preserving the seven hard rules of the Weave.

The user requested synthetic session-history files under the memories schema, treating the collaborative process itself as part of the narrative archive. The immediate purpose was to preserve the route taken into prose writing, not just the destination.
